description.en: Returns a mapping of every enum name to its member, including aliases

info.excerpt: Returns a mapping of every enum name to its member, including aliases

description.en: _ignore_ is only used during creation and is removed from the enumeration once creation is complete. _ignore_ is a list of names that will not become members, and whose names will also be removed from the completed enumeration. See TimePeriod for an example.

info.excerpt: _ignore_ is only used during creation and is removed from the enumeration once creation is complete. _ignore_ is a list of names that will not become members, and whose names will also be removed from...

description.en: Name of the member.

info.excerpt: Name of the member.

description.en: No longer used, kept for backward compatibility. (class attribute, removed during class creation).

info.excerpt: No longer used, kept for backward compatibility. (class attribute, removed during class creation).

description.en: Value of the member, can be set in __new__().

info.excerpt: Value of the member, can be set in __new__().

description.en: By default, does nothing. If multiple values are given in the member assignment, those values become separate arguments to __init__; e.g. >>> from enum import Enum >>> class Weekday(Enum): ... MONDAY = 1, 'Mon' Weekday.__init__() would be called as Weekday.__init__(self, 1, 'Mon')

info.excerpt: By default, does nothing. If multiple values are given in the member assignment, those values become separate arguments to __init__; e.g. >>> from enum import Enum >>> class Weekday(Enum): ... MONDAY ...

description.en: Returns current flag binary and’ed with other: >>> purple & white <Color.RED|BLUE: 5> >>> purple & Color.GREEN <Color: 0>

info.excerpt: Returns current flag binary and’ed with other: >>> purple & white <Color.RED|BLUE: 5> >>> purple & Color.GREEN <Color: 0>

description.en: Returns True if any members in flag, False otherwise: >>> bool(Color.GREEN) True >>> bool(white) True >>> black = Color(0) >>> bool(black) False

info.excerpt: Returns True if any members in flag, False otherwise: >>> bool(Color.GREEN) True >>> bool(white) True >>> black = Color(0) >>> bool(black) False

enum/Type/__call__.name: __call__
signatures: __call__(cls, value, names=None, *, module=None, qualname=None, type=None, start=1, boundary=None)

description.en: This method is called in two different ways: to look up an existing member: cls: The enum class being called. value: The value to lookup. to use the cls enum to create a new enum (only if the existing enum does not have any members): cls: The enum class being called. value: The name of the new Enum to create. names: The names/values of the members for the new Enum. module: The name of the module the new Enum is created in. qualname: The actual location in the module where this Enum can be fou...

info.excerpt: This method is called in two different ways: to look up an existing member: cls: The enum class being called. value: The value to lookup. to use the cls enum to create a new enum (only if the existing...

description.en: Returns True if member belongs to the cls: >>> some_var = Color.RED >>> some_var in Color True >>> Color.RED.value in Color True Changed in version 3.12: Before Python 3.12, a TypeError is raised if a non-Enum-member is used in a containment check.

info.excerpt: Returns True if member belongs to the cls: >>> some_var = Color.RED >>> some_var in Color True >>> Color.RED.value in Color True Changed in version 3.12: Before Python 3.12, a TypeError is raised if a...

description.en: Returns True if value is in self: >>> from enum import Flag, auto >>> class Color(Flag): ... RED = auto() ... GREEN = auto() ... BLUE = auto() ... >>> purple = Color.RED | Color.BLUE >>> white = Color.RED | Color.GREEN | Color.BLUE >>> Color.GREEN in purple False >>> Color.GREEN in white True >>> purple in white True >>> white in purple False

info.excerpt: Returns True if value is in self: >>> from enum import Flag, auto >>> class Color(Flag): ... RED = auto() ... GREEN = auto() ... BLUE = auto() ... >>> purple = Color.RED | Color.BLUE >>> white = Color...

description.en: Returns ['__class__', '__doc__', '__members__', '__module__'] and the names of the members in cls: >>> dir(Color) ['BLUE', 'GREEN', 'RED', '__class__', '__contains__', '__doc__', '__getitem__', '__init_subclass__', '__iter__', '__len__', '__members__', '__module__', '__name__', '__qualname__']

info.excerpt: Returns ['__class__', '__doc__', '__members__', '__module__'] and the names of the members in cls: >>> dir(Color) ['BLUE', 'GREEN', 'RED', '__class__', '__contains__', '__doc__', '__getitem__', '__ini...

description.en: Returns ['__class__', '__doc__', '__module__', 'name', 'value'] and any public methods defined on self.__class__: >>> from enum import Enum >>> from datetime import date >>> class Weekday(Enum): ... MONDAY = 1 ... TUESDAY = 2 ... WEDNESDAY = 3 ... THURSDAY = 4 ... FRIDAY = 5 ... SATURDAY = 6 ... SUNDAY = 7 ... @classmethod ... def today(cls): ... print('today is %s' % cls(date.today().isoweekday()).name) ... >>> dir(Weekday.SATURDAY) ['__class__', '__doc__', '__eq__', '__hash__', '__module__'...

info.excerpt: Returns ['__class__', '__doc__', '__module__', 'name', 'value'] and any public methods defined on self.__class__: >>> from enum import Enum >>> from datetime import date >>> class Weekday(Enum): ... M...

description.en: Returns the string used for format() and f-string calls. By default, returns __str__() return value, but can be overridden: >>> from enum import auto, Enum >>> class OtherStyle(Enum): ... ALTERNATE = auto() ... OTHER = auto() ... SOMETHING_ELSE = auto() ... def __format__(self, spec): ... return f'{self.name}' ... >>> OtherStyle.ALTERNATE, str(OtherStyle.ALTERNATE), f"{OtherStyle.ALTERNATE}" (<OtherStyle.ALTERNATE: 1>, 'OtherStyle.ALTERNATE', 'ALTERNATE')

info.excerpt: Returns the string used for format() and f-string calls. By default, returns __str__() return value, but can be overridden: >>> from enum import auto, Enum >>> class OtherStyle(Enum): ... ALTERNATE = ...

description.en: Returns the Enum member in cls matching name, or raises a KeyError: >>> Color['BLUE'] <Color.BLUE: 3>

info.excerpt: Returns the Enum member in cls matching name, or raises a KeyError: >>> Color['BLUE'] <Color.BLUE: 3>

description.en: A classmethod that is used to further configure subsequent subclasses. By default, does nothing.

info.excerpt: A classmethod that is used to further configure subsequent subclasses. By default, does nothing.

description.en: Returns all the flags in type(self) that are not in self: >>> ~white <Color: 0> >>> ~purple <Color.GREEN: 2> >>> ~Color.RED <Color.GREEN|BLUE: 6>

info.excerpt: Returns all the flags in type(self) that are not in self: >>> ~white <Color: 0> >>> ~purple <Color.GREEN: 2> >>> ~Color.RED <Color.GREEN|BLUE: 6>

description.en: Returns each member in cls in definition order: >>> list(Color) [<Color.RED: 1>, <Color.GREEN: 2>, <Color.BLUE: 3>]

info.excerpt: Returns each member in cls in definition order: >>> list(Color) [<Color.RED: 1>, <Color.GREEN: 2>, <Color.BLUE: 3>]

description.en: Returns all contained non-alias members: >>> list(Color.RED) [<Color.RED: 1>] >>> list(purple) [<Color.RED: 1>, <Color.BLUE: 4>] Added in version 3.11.

info.excerpt: Returns all contained non-alias members: >>> list(Color.RED) [<Color.RED: 1>] >>> list(purple) [<Color.RED: 1>, <Color.BLUE: 4>] Added in version 3.11.

description.en: Returns the number of member in cls: >>> len(Color) 3

info.excerpt: Returns the number of member in cls: >>> len(Color) 3

description.en: Returns number of members in flag: >>> len(Color.GREEN) 1 >>> len(white) 3 Added in version 3.11.

info.excerpt: Returns number of members in flag: >>> len(Color.GREEN) 1 >>> len(white) 3 Added in version 3.11.

description.en: By default, doesn’t exist. If specified, either in the enum class definition or in a mixin class (such as int), all values given in the member assignment will be passed; e.g. >>> from enum import Enum >>> class MyIntEnum(int, Enum): ... TWENTYSIX = '1a', 16 results in the call int('1a', 16) and a value of 26 for the member. Note When writing a custom __new__, do not use super().__new__ – call the appropriate __new__ instead.

info.excerpt: By default, doesn’t exist. If specified, either in the enum class definition or in a mixin class (such as int), all values given in the member assignment will be passed; e.g. >>> from enum import Enum...

description.en: Returns current flag binary or’ed with other: >>> Color.RED | Color.GREEN <Color.RED|GREEN: 3>

info.excerpt: Returns current flag binary or’ed with other: >>> Color.RED | Color.GREEN <Color.RED|GREEN: 3>

description.en: Returns the string used for repr() calls. By default, returns the Enum name, member name, and value, but can be overridden: >>> from enum import auto, Enum >>> class OtherStyle(Enum): ... ALTERNATE = auto() ... OTHER = auto() ... SOMETHING_ELSE = auto() ... def __repr__(self): ... cls_name = self.__class__.__name__ ... return f'{cls_name}.{self.name}' ... >>> OtherStyle.ALTERNATE, str(OtherStyle.ALTERNATE), f"{OtherStyle.ALTERNATE}" (OtherStyle.ALTERNATE, 'OtherStyle.ALTERNATE', 'OtherStyle.A...

info.excerpt: Returns the string used for repr() calls. By default, returns the Enum name, member name, and value, but can be overridden: >>> from enum import auto, Enum >>> class OtherStyle(Enum): ... ALTERNATE = ...

description.en: Returns each member in cls in reverse definition order: >>> list(reversed(Color)) [<Color.BLUE: 3>, <Color.GREEN: 2>, <Color.RED: 1>]

info.excerpt: Returns each member in cls in reverse definition order: >>> list(reversed(Color)) [<Color.BLUE: 3>, <Color.GREEN: 2>, <Color.RED: 1>]

description.en: Returns the string used for str() calls. By default, returns the Enum name and member name, but can be overridden: >>> from enum import auto, Enum >>> class OtherStyle(Enum): ... ALTERNATE = auto() ... OTHER = auto() ... SOMETHING_ELSE = auto() ... def __str__(self): ... return f'{self.name}' ... >>> OtherStyle.ALTERNATE, str(OtherStyle.ALTERNATE), f"{OtherStyle.ALTERNATE}" (<OtherStyle.ALTERNATE: 1>, 'ALTERNATE', 'ALTERNATE')

info.excerpt: Returns the string used for str() calls. By default, returns the Enum name and member name, but can be overridden: >>> from enum import auto, Enum >>> class OtherStyle(Enum): ... ALTERNATE = auto() .....

description.en: Returns current flag binary xor’ed with other: >>> purple ^ white <Color.GREEN: 2> >>> purple ^ Color.GREEN <Color.RED|GREEN|BLUE: 7>

info.excerpt: Returns current flag binary xor’ed with other: >>> purple ^ white <Color.GREEN: 2> >>> purple ^ Color.GREEN <Color.RED|GREEN|BLUE: 7>

description.en: Adds a new name as an alias to an existing member: >>> Color.RED._add_alias_("ERROR") >>> Color.ERROR <Color.RED: 1> Raises a NameError if the name is already assigned to a different member. Added in version 3.13.

info.excerpt: Adds a new name as an alias to an existing member: >>> Color.RED._add_alias_("ERROR") >>> Color.ERROR <Color.RED: 1> Raises a NameError if the name is already assigned to a different member. Added in ...

description.en: Adds a new value as an alias to an existing member: >>> Color.RED._add_value_alias_(42) >>> Color(42) <Color.RED: 1> Raises a ValueError if the value is already linked with a different member. Added in version 3.13.

info.excerpt: Adds a new value as an alias to an existing member: >>> Color.RED._add_value_alias_(42) >>> Color(42) <Color.RED: 1> Raises a ValueError if the value is already linked with a different member. Added i...

description.en: name: The name of the member being defined (e.g. ‘RED’). start: The start value for the Enum; the default is 1. count: The number of members currently defined, not including this one. last_values: A list of the previous values. A staticmethod that is used to determine the next value returned by auto: >>> from enum import auto, Enum >>> class PowersOfThree(Enum): ... @staticmethod ... def _generate_next_value_(name, start, count, last_values): ... return 3 ** (count + 1) ... FIRST = auto() ......

description.en: A classmethod for looking up values not found in cls. By default it does nothing, but can be overridden to implement custom search behavior: >>> from enum import auto, StrEnum >>> class Build(StrEnum): ... DEBUG = auto() ... OPTIMIZED = auto() ... @classmethod ... def _missing_(cls, value): ... value = value.lower() ... for member in cls: ... if member.value == value: ... return member ... return None ... >>> Build.DEBUG.value 'debug' >>> Build('deBUG') <Build.DEBUG: 'debug'>

info.excerpt: A classmethod for looking up values not found in cls. By default it does nothing, but can be overridden to implement custom search behavior: >>> from enum import auto, StrEnum >>> class Build(StrEnum)...

description.en: Function used to format any remaining unnamed numeric values. Default is the value’s repr; common choices are hex() and oct().

info.excerpt: Function used to format any remaining unnamed numeric values. Default is the value’s repr; common choices are hex() and oct().
